title: Four internal logDecision calls carry no tags, so they throw in any repo with a decision taxonomy
action: REPRODUCED: `logDecision` refuses with typed DECISIONS_UNTAGGED_REFUSED when `docs/decisions/taxonomy.json` exists and the event carries zero tags (decision-propagation D7b). Four INTERNAL call sites pass no `tags` at all — census run, all four confirmed: `.bee/bin/lib/claims.mjs:851` (sweep resets a stale claim), `.bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs:1423` (cap with --override-judge), `:2019` (reset-budget), `:2129` (judge-record reopening a capped cell on NEEDS_REVISION). Each throw unwinds the WHOLE surrounding write transaction, so the operation fails — this is why okf-switchover-f3's NEEDS_REVISION verdict had to be hand-written into the decisions log and a P1 filed. This repo HAS a taxonomy, so all four are live here. FIX: give each call an accurate `tags` array drawn from the EXISTING taxonomy vocabulary (`node -e "console.log(require('./docs/decisions/taxonomy.json').tags.map(t=>t.name).join(' '))"` — do not invent new tag names; `claims`, `cells`, `judge`, `sessions`, `sweep`, `decisions` are all present). Tag each for what the event IS, not generically: the sweep is claims+sweep, the override is cells+judge, the budget reset is cells, the reopen is cells+judge. REGRESSION COVERAGE is the point, not just the fix — the class here is 'a cross-cutting write-time rule shipped without sweeping its existing internal callers'. Add a suite (extend the nearest existing one — check `skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_cells.mjs` and `test_claims.mjs` first) that runs each of the four operations in a fixture repo that HAS a taxonomy and asserts each SUCCEEDS and lands a tagged event. Then add ONE census assertion that fails if any future `logDecision(` call inside `.bee/bin/lib/**` or `templates/lib/**` omits `tags` — derived by scanning the source, never a hand-maintained list of line numbers, so the next cross-cutting rule cannot ship past its own callers. MIRROR: `.bee/bin/lib/**` and `skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/**` must stay byte-identical — edit the template copy and mirror, or edit both; `scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs` enforces it. Then run the four derived-artifact regens (mirror, plugin render, release manifest, self-onboard) since templates/lib is touched.

truths: red evidence: each of the four operations shown FAILING in a taxonomy fixture before the fix, with the typed code quoted
green evidence: each of the four SUCCEEDS after, and the logged event carries its tags
the census assertion fails when a tagless logDecision call is injected into a lib file, and passes on the fixed tree
no new tag names invented — every tag used already exists in docs/decisions/taxonomy.json tags[]
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
prohibitions: never weaken or bypass the untagged refusal itself — the rule is correct, the callers were not swept
never pass a generic catch-all tag to satisfy the check; the tag must describe the event
do not hand-maintain a list of call-site line numbers — derive the census from the source

trace.outcome: Four internal decision-log callers carried no tags, so in any repo with a decision taxonomy — this one included — three of them threw and unwound their whole write transaction, and the fourth silently dropped its audit line inside a pre-existing catch. That is why an earlier feature's NEEDS_REVISION verdict had to be hand-written. Fixed with tags drawn from the existing vocabulary, none invented, each naming what the event is. The real deliverable is the regression net: a census assertion that DERIVES its call sites by scanning the library source and fails if any future decision-log call there omits tags — proven to bite by stripping a real tags line and watching it name the exact file and line. The defect class was a cross-cutting write-time rule shipped without sweeping the callers already in the tree; the census is what makes that unrepeatable. A fifth tagless caller outside this cell's scope was found and flagged rather than quietly swept in.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "Reproduced twice — once by the orchestrator before the cell was written (a temp fixture: no taxonomy -> logDecision succeeds; with taxonomy -> throws DECISIONS_UNTAGGED_REFUSED), and once per-operation by the worker's new tests against the pre-fix tree. capCell --override-judge threw before ever writing judge_overrides or capping; resetCellBudget threw before the budget write; recordJudgeVerdict's NEEDS_REVISION reopen threw before writeCell, so even the semantic_judge entry never landed. The census scanner flagged exactly 8 offending lines (4 sites x 2 mirrored trees).",
  "worker_corrected_my_diagnosis": "The cell asserted all four throws 'unwind the whole surrounding write transaction'. True for three. The fourth, claims.mjs:851, sits inside a PRE-EXISTING try/catch ('best-effort — the cell reset above already committed'), so its real pre-fix failure mode was a SILENTLY DROPPED audit line: the sweep succeeded and activeDecisions returned [] for the cell. Arguably the worse of the two modes, because nothing surfaces. The worker measured it, reported the correction rather than restating my wording, and tested for the behavior that actually occurs.",
  "green_evidence": "All four operations now succeed and land a tagged event: override-judge ['cells','judge'], reset-budget ['cells'], judge-record reopen ['cells','judge'], sweep-reset ['claims','sweep']. test_cells.mjs 103 passed, test_claims.mjs 25 passed.",
  "the_actual_deliverable": "The four-line fix is not the point; the regression net is. The defect class is 'a cross-cutting write-time rule shipped without sweeping its existing internal callers' — decision-propagation D7b added the refusal and nobody swept the callers already in the tree. The new census assertion derives its call sites by SCANNING THE SOURCE of .bee/bin/lib/** and templates/lib/**, never a hand-maintained list of line numbers, and fails if any future logDecision call there omits tags.",
  "census_proven_to_bite": "Two ways. Unit-level: findLogDecisionCalls flags a synthetic tagless call, accepts a tagged one, and correctly ignores the `function logDecision(` declaration itself. Real-source: the worker stripped the tags line from cells.mjs:1429, re-ran, and the census failed naming `.bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs:1423` exactly; restored, re-ran green, and re-mirrored with byte-identity confirmed.",
  "tags_not_invented": "claims, sweep, cells, judge — every one already present in docs/decisions/taxonomy.json tags[], confirmed by script. Each applied for what the event actually is, never a generic catch-all to satisfy the check.",
  "known_gap_out_of_scope": "The worker found a FIFTH tagless internal caller — `.bee/bin/bee.mjs:1879`, the scribing-debt-waiver record on feature close — same bug class, live in this repo, and OUTSIDE both this cell's census scope (.bee/bin/lib/** and templates/lib/**) and its files list. Correctly left untouched and flagged rather than silently swept in. The scope was too narrow because the orchestrator wrote it that way; that is its own cell, not a quiet edit here.",
  "chain": "Re-run INDEPENDENTLY by the orchestrator, both ways: BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 81 suite(s) exit 0; node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 81 suite(s) exit 0. Mirror byte-identity between .bee/bin/lib and templates/lib confirmed by direct diff on both touched files.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Tags verified present at all four sites with an explanatory comment naming the failure mode; mirror diffed clean; census assertion confirmed live in the suite. The fifth site read directly and confirmed genuine: it is the audit record for the sanctioned scribing-debt waiver, so a throw there would leave a feature close half-done."
}
